zh.lifeSummary: William de Bertram於大約1020年出生在法國諾門迪。他與Gizelle Bostenburgh在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1086年在他的家鄉享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William de Bertram于大约1020年出生在法国諾門迪。他与Gizelle Bostenburgh在法国諾門迪结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1086年在他的家乡享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William de Bertram was born about 1020, in Normandy, France. He married Gizelle Bostenburgh in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1086, in his hometown, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Bertram est né environ 1020, à Normandie, France. Il a épousé Gizelle Bostenburgh à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1086, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William de Bertram wurde um 1020 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hat in Normandie, Frankreich Gizelle Bostenburgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1086 im Alter von 67 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: William de Bertram è nato il circa 1020 nel Normandie, Francia. Lui sposato Gizelle Bostenburgh nel Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1086, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Bertramは約1020年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた。フランスノーマンディでGizelle Bostenburghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1086年に故郷で67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Bertram(은)는 대략 1020년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그는 프랑스 노먼디에서 Gizelle Bostenburgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1086년 그의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William de Bertram nasceu aproximadamente 1020, em Normandie, França. Ele casou-se com Gizelle Bostenburgh em Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1086, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William de Bertram родился около 1020 г. в местности норманди, Франция. Он женился на женщине по имени Gizelle Bostenburgh в местности норманди, Франция. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1086 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William de Bertram nació aproximadamente 1020, en Normandie, Francia. Se casó con Gizelle Bostenburgh en Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1086, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años.
